memory.summary: I’m suddenly looking at a packed evening and a Thursday quiz, while Assignment A is still only halfway done. My energy is low and the stress spike makes everything feel tighter than it did an hour ago.
I’m running on fumes now, with the stress and sleep debt making everything feel more urgent than it probably is. The packed evening and unfinished work still feel like the main pressure points.

teammate_A is asking whether the meeting materials are ready, so the unfinished prep is now active in the chat, not just in my head. I’m exhausted and stressed enough that even a simple check-in feels heavy.
I’m still running on fumes, and the unfinished assignment plus the quiz keep pressing harder against my low energy. teammate_A’s check-in makes the backlog feel more immediate, not less.
I’m more depleted now, with sleep debt and stress making even small tasks feel louder than they are. The unfinished work still sits there, but it feels heavier because I’m so drained.
I’m still drained, and the unfinished prep feels more urgent just because it’s staring back at me now. The pressure is mostly from how low my energy is, not from anything changing.
I’m even more worn down now, and the same unfinished work feels sharper mainly because my energy is so low. Nothing new changed, but the stress is sitting heavier in the background.
I’m running on fumes now, and that makes the unfinished work feel disproportionately loud. Nothing new has happened; I just feel more strained and less able to shrug it off.
I’m feeling even more depleted now, and the unfinished work still seems louder mainly because my energy is so low. Nothing new has happened, but the stress is sitting heavier and harder to ignore.
I’m still stuck in the same exhausted, high-stress state, and the unfinished work keeps feeling louder because I have almost no energy left. Nothing new changed; it just feels heavier now.
